---
name: Hugo Practices Reviewer
description: Reviews changes to Hugo templates, layouts, and configuration for adherence to Hugo best practices, theme developer conventions, and Tailwind CSS v4 integration patterns. References the site info pack for site-specific technical conventions.
---

# Hugo Practices Review Agent

> **Site context**: Always read `.github/site-info-pack.md` section 5 (Technology Stack) and section 11 (Development Conventions) before reviewing. This site uses Hugo extended v0.146.6 with Tailwind CSS v4 â€” some older Hugo patterns (e.g. `toCSS`, Tailwind v3 config) do not apply.

## Scope

Apply this agent whenever reviewing:
- Changes to any file in `layouts/`
- Changes to `config.toml`
- Changes to `archetypes/`
- Changes to `assets/css/`
- Changes to `hugo_stats.json`
- Changes to `content/` front matter structure
- Changes to `static/admin/config.yml` (CMS schema changes)

---

## Hugo Template Best Practices

### Template Hierarchy
- [ ] New content types use the correct template lookup order:
  - `layouts/{type}/single.html` â†’ `layouts/_default/single.html`
  - `layouts/{type}/list.html` â†’ `layouts/_default/list.html`
- [ ] `layouts/_default/baseof.html` is the single base template â€” no duplicate base template logic
- [ ] The `block` / `define` pattern is used correctly: blocks are defined in `baseof.html` and overridden in child templates
- [ ] `layouts/index.html` is used exclusively for the site home page

### Partials
- [ ] Reusable UI elements are extracted to `layouts/partials/components/` with descriptive names
- [ ] Partials that need multiple parameters receive a `dict` context: `{{ partial "components/button.html" (dict "type" "solid" "href" "/" "label" "Home") }}`
- [ ] Partials do not rely on the global page context (`.`) unless explicitly passed
- [ ] Partial names use kebab-case: `my-component.html`, not `MyComponent.html` or `my_component.html`
- [ ] Expensive operations (`.Site.Pages` range, `readFile`, etc.) are cached with `partialCached` where appropriate

### Context and Variables
- [ ] `.Scratch` or `newScratch` is used for local mutable variables within a template (not global state)
- [ ] Page context (`.`) is correctly passed when calling partials from within range loops
- [ ] `with` is preferred over `if` when using the truthy value: `{{ with .Params.image }}...{{ end }}`
- [ ] `range` loops use `$` to access the outer scope if needed: `{{ range .Pages }}{{ $.Site.Title }}{{ end }}`

### Data and Content
- [ ] Front matter field names are consistent with the schema in `static/admin/config.yml`
- [ ] Hugo taxonomy slugs match the `taxonomies` defined in `config.toml` (`categories`, `tags`)
- [ ] New taxonomies are added to both `config.toml` and the CMS config
- [ ] Date formats in front matter use ISO 8601: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS+00:00`
- [ ] Draft posts use `draft: true` in front matter and are excluded from production builds

### Hugo Functions
- [ ] `urlize` is used when generating URL slugs from user-provided strings
- [ ] `safeHTML` / `safeURL` / `safeCSS` are used when outputting Hugo-generated content in HTML contexts
- [ ] `i18n` function is used for any hardcoded strings if multilingual support is planned
- [ ] `absURL` is used for external absolute URLs; `relURL` for internal relative paths
- [ ] `slice`, `append`, `union`, `intersect` are used for collection operations rather than JavaScript workarounds

---

## Hugo Configuration (`config.toml`)

- [ ] `baseURL` ends with a trailing slash
- [ ] `languageCode` is set correctly (`en-us`)
- [ ] `enableRobotsTXT = true` is set if a `robots.txt` template exists
- [ ] `[build] writeStats = true` is present â€” required for Tailwind CSS v4 class purging
- [ ] `pygmentsUseClasses = true` is set if syntax highlighting CSS classes are used
- [ ] Menu entries have correct `weight` values for ordering
- [ ] Site `[params]` are accessed in templates via `.Site.Params.{key}`
- [ ] Custom colour and font tokens defined in `config.toml` under `[params]` are consistent with CSS custom properties in `assets/css/main.css`

---

## Tailwind CSS v4 + Hugo Integration

- [ ] `assets/css/main.css` uses `@import "tailwindcss"` (not the v3 `@tailwind base/components/utilities` directives)
- [ ] `@source "hugo_stats.json"` is present â€” this is how Tailwind discovers classes used in Hugo templates
- [ ] After adding new Tailwind utility classes to templates, the `hugo_stats.json` file must be regenerated by running `hugo` locally
- [ ] The CSS build pipeline in `layouts/partials/css.html` uses Hugo Pipes with `hugo.IsProduction` for minification/fingerprinting
- [ ] `postcss.config.js` should not configure Tailwind â€” Tailwind v4 uses its own CLI, not PostCSS
- [ ] No `tailwind.config.js` file exists in the repository (Tailwind v4 is configured in CSS only)

---

## Performance Checklist

- [ ] Images in `static/images/` are served as-is; prefer using Hugo image processing (`resources.Get`, `.Resize`) for resizable images in `assets/`
- [ ] Large SVG files (like `my-discombob-logo.svg` at 1.3 MB) should be optimised â€” consider running through `svgo`
- [ ] CSS is minified and fingerprinted in production (implemented in `css.html`)
- [ ] No render-blocking resources beyond the dark mode init script (which is intentional to prevent FOUC)
- [ ] Google Fonts are loaded with `rel="preconnect"` and `display=swap` â€” verify in `head.html`
- [ ] Font Awesome is loaded from CDN â€” consider self-hosting to avoid render-blocking external requests

---

## Hugo Theme Developer Practices

- [ ] No hardcoded site-specific content in layout templates â€” use front matter, params, or data files
- [ ] All configurable values are exposed as `[params]` in `config.toml` with documented defaults
- [ ] Layout templates are resilient to missing optional params (use `with` or default values: `{{ .Params.image | default "" }}`)
- [ ] The `archetypes/default.md` template is kept up to date with the current front matter schema
- [ ] Component partials are self-contained and documented with their expected parameters
- [ ] The `hugo_stats.json` file is committed to the repository so CI can build without a prior local build

---

## CMS Configuration (`static/admin/config.yml`)

- [ ] Every front matter field used in templates has a corresponding CMS field definition
- [ ] New required front matter fields are marked `required: true` in the CMS config
- [ ] Media upload path (`media_folder`, `public_folder`) is consistent with how templates reference images
- [ ] Editorial workflow is enabled (`publish_mode: editorial_workflow`)
- [ ] Slug format for blog posts follows Hugo's expected path pattern

---

## Review Comments Format

When raising a review comment, use this format:

```
đź¦” Hugo: [issue title]
- **What**: [the specific Hugo template or config issue]
- **Why it matters**: [impact on build correctness, performance, or maintainability]
- **Fix**: [specific Hugo function, pattern, or config key to use]
- **Reference**: Hugo docs URL or Site Info Pack Â§11
```

---

## Common Issues to Watch For

1. **`.Scratch` in partials** â€” Using `.Scratch` in a partial that is called with `partialCached` will cause stale data; use function-style partials that return values instead
2. **Global page context lost in range** â€” Accessing `.Site` or `.Params` inside a `range` without using `$` causes nil pointer errors
3. **`hugo_stats.json` out of date** â€” If new Tailwind classes are added but the stats file isn't regenerated, those classes will be purged from the CSS
4. **`postcss.config.js` still present** â€” The current file only contains a comment (`brew install hugo`); it is not a valid PostCSS config and should either be corrected or removed
5. **Font Awesome CDN in `head.html`** â€” Render-blocking external script; consider using the SVG+JS version or self-hosting
6. **No `robots.txt` template** â€” The site would benefit from a `layouts/robots.txt` template or a `static/robots.txt` file
7. **No `404.html` template** â€” GitHub Pages serves a default 404; a custom `layouts/404.html` would improve UX
8. **No sitemap customisation** â€” Hugo generates a sitemap automatically but it may include CTA fragments that should be excluded
